rows.notes: Clear, centered page title. No amounts present. Vertical rulings not needed for this header.
Clear section heading under the title. No monetary amounts on this line.
Pounds/shillings/pence columns located by projecting final total line; reading 66 13 6 is well aligned. Minor uncertainty in tiny pence flourish but digits are legible.
Clear pounds (three-digit), shillings and pence align with ruling lines. Handwritten 'Do' phrase above is unambiguous. Minor slant of page but numbers conform to column boundaries.

Pounds read as 234, shillings '8' and pence '6' align with projected ruling lines. There is slight ambiguity on the shillings digit shape (noted in page guidance); chosen '8' because its alignment with column and comparison with similar digit shapes on page favors 8.

Pounds clearly '7'. The shillings digit is small and the pence stroke is slightly smudged — read as 4s 6d based on alignment with ruling and nearby similar digits. Moderate ambiguity due to faint ink.
Section label introducing numbered subitems (1, 2, 3). No direct amounts in heading itself.

This line is intentionally struck-through on the original page (faint but visible stroke across text). Amount read as 22 3 0 from projected columns; struck-through marking reduces confidence. Chose pounds/shillings/pence by alignment with the prominent total line and nearby numeric format.

Also struck-through (visible ink line through text). The descriptive text is partially obscured by the strike. Amount aligned to columns reads 11 8 6; some ambiguity from strike-through and cramped handwriting in this block, lowering confidence.
Legible '3' and word 'Nil' indicating no amount for this sub-item. Column projection confirms no figures present.
Description is legible; no monetary figures appear on this line. Alignment with ruling lines confirms blank amount fields.
Clear numeric '43 0 0' aligned under pounds/shillings/pence. Description legible; columns unambiguous.

Amount reads '1 14 4' with a small trailing loop after the pence that resembles 'ob' (halfpenny). Projected columns place these digits firmly in pounds/shillings/pence; fraction interpreted as 0.5 (halfpenny). Minor ambiguity in tiny fraction symbol but loop matches 'ob' elsewhere on page.
Clear '2 10 0' aligned to ruling lines. 'Capitale' spelling as written; digits unambiguous.

rows.description: Int on £100 at 4 per cent Augt 18 1880 by Do
rows.notes: Numeric 4 0 0 clear and aligned. Text has common abbreviations 'Do' referring to earlier cognate.

rows.description: Int on £150 at Do to April 1 1880 by Do
rows.notes: Clear reading '6 0 0' in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Alignment strong.
Amount '13 2 10' is legible though the '2' shillings digit has a slight flourish. Column alignment supports chosen digits.

rows.description: Int for one yr to Domus for £500 by Bursar
rows.notes: Clear '15 0 0' in the monetary columns; description legible. No ambiguity.
Margin annotation 'July' extracted as a separate header/annotation (per page guidance). Clearly in the left margin and not part of the main description.
Pounds '114', shillings '4', pence '3' read from columns projected upward from total. Slight ink blotting near pence but digits remain within valid range.
Clear line reading '10 0 0' at right. This is the earlier 'Bourse Register' entry (distinct from a later small register entry).
Monetary style '122.15.0' written in the main text; alignment confirms 122 pounds, 15 shillings, 0 pence. Slight dot between groups but standard format.
Reads '93.3.4' under the Bank/Cash group; digits align with ruling lines and are clear.
Reads '46.19.8'. The middle shillings '19' is close to the column separator and slightly cramped; validated against allowed range (0-19). Good alignment.

rows.description: Stock of the three funds sold
rows.notes: Reads '606.17.4'. Pounds are three digits and clearly in the pound column. Slight ink fade but digits fall squarely into columns.
Reads '347.4.0' and a check-mark adjacent. Well aligned to the pounds/shillings/pence rulings. This entry appears to be the last of the left-hand block.
Margin annotation 'July 1' extracted separately as instructed. Clearly in left margin near the following small items.
Clear small entry '10 0 0' under July 1. Description handwritten but legible; amount columns unambiguous.
Reads '25 0 0'. Surname in text is a little unclear (interpreted as 'Hammond'); chosen reading based on letter shapes. Amount unambiguous.
Final total at bottom right used as the alignment reference for the page. Pounds/shillings/pence are clear and served to project column boundaries upward for all rows.
